mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment indicates weakening labor market, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE on 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05) or the mechanism produces positive returns in >50% of periods with rising UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, increasing borrowing costs and discount rates, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 5%, average 20-day S&P 500 returns are non-negative or not significantly different from periods below 5% (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Combined strength in GDP (growth) and retail sales (consumer demand) signals robust economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and pushes equities higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both GDP and retail sales are above their respective 6-month trends, future 60-day S&P 500 returns do not exceed the unconditional mean, or the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In low-inflation environments (CPI YoY < 3%), declining unemployment boosts consumer confidence and spending, positively impacting equity markets. In high-inflation environments, the same decline signals overheating and triggers tightening fears, producing negative returns. The hypothesis is evaluated under the low-inflation regime.
failure_condition: In low inflation regimes, negative unemployment changes do not lead to significantly positive 20-day returns (t-test p>0.05), or in high inflation regimes the sign does not flip to negative (i.e., returns remain positive).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicate strong manufacturing activity, which correlates with rising corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: After INDPRO growth >0.5% MoM, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is not greater than zero (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals imminent labor market weakness, triggering negative short-term equity sentiment and negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Following a negative PAYEMS print, the 5-day S&P 500 return does not have a median less than zero (Wilcoxon test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI and FEDFUNDS indicate that the Fed is hiking in response to rising inflation, compressing valuation multiples and causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise sequentially, future 60-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative (lower tail test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) boost economic confidence and construction-related stocks, positive for equities, but only when mortgage rates are low. In high-rate environments, rising starts can signal overbuilding and future corrections. This hypothesis is evaluated in a low mortgage rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 3%).
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 3%, positive HOUST changes do not lead to above-median 20-day returns (rank-sum test p>0.10).
